page: Vultr vcg-a16-6c-64g-16vram is a GPU-accelerated virtual server belonging to the Cloud GPU family. It features an Intel Broadwell x86_64 architecture processor with 6 physical cores and 6 vCPUs running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, and it is configured with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 10.67 GB per core. Graphics processing is handled by a single NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage consists of a 350 GB NVMe SSD.

Performance benchmarks place this server in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 1745.38, and the multi-core score is 10619.81. Passmark CPU testing yields a score of 10693.5, with an encryption test score of 3604.96 and a physics test score of 1498.67. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 586.08 GB/sec and a large block uncached read speed of 76.25 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance is weak, scoring 30.39 GB/sec, which represents a potential bottleneck for parallelized decompression tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a balanced entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads by combining a mid-range shared Intel CPU with a dedicated NVIDIA Ampere GPU and a generous 64.0 GB memory allocation. The shared CPU allocation and older Broadwell architecture represent a performance tradeoff compared to dedicated, newer-generation processors, but this is offset by the high resource density of the system memory and local NVMe storage.

This configuration is suitable for workloads requiring GPU acceleration with moderate CPU demands. Appropriate use cases include entry-level machine learning model training, video transcoding, graphics rendering, and database operations that benefit from GPU acceleration and 64.0 GB of system memory.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-6c-64g-16vram is a GPU-accelerated virtual server designed for workloads requiring dedicated graphics processing and substantial system memory. It is equipped with an Intel Broadwell x86_64 processor featuring 6 shared vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory and a 350 GB NVMe SSD. The primary accelerator is a single NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. Benchmark results indicate average performance across standard compute and memory operations, with a Passmark CPU score of 10693.5 and a Redis SET throughput of 10.78 million operations per second. However, multi-threaded decompression tasks show weaker performance. This server provides a cost-effective option for memory-intensive GPU workloads, making it suitable for entry-level machine learning, video transcoding, and database applications.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-6c-64g-16vram is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 6 shared Intel vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, a 350 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 16 GB VRAM for entry-level AI and media workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-6c-64g-16vram is a GPU-accelerated server with 6 Intel vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 16 GB VRAM.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual server featuring an NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU, 64 GB of memory, and local NVMe storage for media and AI workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with one NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 16 GB VRAM
Features 6 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory
Includes 350 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers average CPU and memory bandwidth performance in benchmarks
Suitable for entry-level machine learning, video transcoding, and database tasks
